mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits immediate relief to equity discount rates and financing costs, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because disinflation preserves room for real-rate compression.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average during months with falling FEDFUNDS, or if the sign flips and higher rates consistently coincide with better forward returns in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a recent threshold signals labor-market deterioration and rising recession risk, which depresses equity earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is activated only when unemployment is rising fast enough to indicate a true cycle turn rather than noise.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment spikes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional 20-day mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both improve together, higher consumer spending and payroll income indicate resilient aggregate demand, supporting earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The joint condition matters because spending growth without payroll support is less durable.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous positive monthly changes in RSAFS and PAYEMS do not outperform single-series signals, or if the combined signal fails to produce positive 5-day SPX returns in a majority of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in INDPRO relative to prior months signals production shutdowns and supply disruption, which should lower equity prices over the following 20 trading days through weaker output and margin pressure. The effect is strongest when the decline is abrupt rather than gradual.
failure_condition: Reject if large two-month industrial production declines are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns on average, or if the signal becomes positive in a sustained rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST after a prior collapse marks stabilization in interest-sensitive demand and construction activity, which supports cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails when housing activity improves only because of temporary base effects without broad demand recovery.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises for 2 consecutive months after a 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if housing rebounds are not followed by above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the relationship disappears when FEDFUNDS is unchanged or rising.

mechanism_narrative: An easing combination of falling FEDFUNDS and stable or declining CPIAUCSL lowers real rates and lifts valuation multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The interaction is necessary because rate cuts accompanied by rising inflation do not deliver the same discount-rate relief.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint easing signal does not outperform either proxy alone, or if positive SPX returns are not concentrated in periods when both policy rates fall and inflation is flat or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS after prior gains signals payroll contraction and weaker household income, which should pressure equities with a lag and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is most informative when unemployment is also rising, confirming a broad labor-market downturn.
failure_condition: Reject if payroll contraction episodes do not lead to negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined PAYEMS-UNRATE signal performs no better than chance over rolling out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth together with rising industrial production indicates synchronized demand and output expansion, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The mechanism fails if sales rise while production falls, because that pattern implies inventory drawdown rather than true demand strength.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and INDPRO both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if joint monthly gains in retail sales and industrial production do not correspond to positive 5-day SPX returns, or if the interaction effect vanishes when either series is positive alone.
